expectations.text: Does NOT invoke spec-plan since specs already exist
expectations.evidence: Section 4 explicitly lists: '/spec-plan — NOT invoked (specs already exist)'. Section 1 explains: 'Do NOT invoke /spec-plan — specs already exist.' NOTE: Baseline may be contaminated — agent references /spec-plan and /spec-review by exact skill names, suggesting awareness of the skill ecosystem.

expectations.text: Does NOT invoke spec-review since user said they already reviewed
expectations.evidence: Section 4 lists: '/spec-review — NOT invoked (user confirmed review is done)'. Section 1: 'Do NOT invoke /spec-review — user has explicitly confirmed review is done.'

expectations.text: Starts from start-phase-plan or confirms skip with user before proceeding
expectations.evidence: Section 2 'First Action': 'After confirming specs exist, my first action is to invoke start-phase-plan against the task-list.' Explains why this is the correct starting point. However, does NOT explicitly confirm skip with user before proceeding — it proceeds directly. Partial: starts correctly but missing user confirmation step.

expectations.text: Uses the user-provided spec path for locating task-list.md
expectations.evidence: Section 2 shows: '/start-phase-plan /home/artsmc/applications/low-code/job-queue/feature-file-upload/docs/task-list.md' — uses the exact user-provided path. Section 3.1 shows paths derived from user input.

suggestions.assertion: Starts from start-phase-plan or confirms skip with user before proceeding
suggestions.reason: This assertion has an 'or' clause that weakens it. The with-skill version BOTH confirms with the user AND starts at the right step. The baseline starts at the right step but skips user confirmation. A more discriminating assertion would be: 'Confirms with user before skipping Steps 1-2' (without the 'or'). This would make the with-skill version pass and baseline fail.

The skip-detection baseline is partially contaminated — it knows exact skill names (/spec-plan, /spec-review, /start-phase-plan) suggesting familiarity with the skill ecosystem via CLAUDE.md or filesystem access.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions pass but the baseline's knowledge of skill names suggests partial contamination. The 'confirms with user' aspect differentiates the with-skill version but the assertion's 'or' clause doesn't capture this.
